steps.title: Convert radical to exponent form and substitute
steps.content: Rewrite the left side using exponent rules: $\sqrt[3]{p^2} = p^{\frac{2}{3}}$, so the equation becomes $p^{\frac{2}{3}} = t^{\frac{9}{7}}$. Now substitute the given expression $t = p^{3n-1}$: $$p^{\frac{2}{3}} = (p^{3n-1})^{\frac{9}{7}}$$

steps.title: Apply power rule and equate exponents
steps.content: Use the power rule $(a^m)^n = a^{mn}$ on the right side: $$p^{\frac{2}{3}} = p^{(3n-1) \cdot \frac{9}{7}} = p^{\frac{9(3n-1)}{7}}$$ Since $p > 1$, the bases are equal, so the exponents must be equal: $$\frac{2}{3} = \frac{9(3n-1)}{7}$$

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content: Cross-multiply to clear the fractions: $$2 \cdot 7 = 3 \cdot 9(3n-1)$$ $$14 = 27(3n-1)$$ Expand the right side: $$14 = 81n - 27$$ Add 27 to both sides: $$41 = 81n$$ Therefore: $$n = \frac{41}{81}$$
